rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 1, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted, conditioned on the following terms that are just pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(a): If Weyerhaueser Company ("WC") and/or International Paper Company ("IP") serve written discovery on plaintiffs, the time to respond is shortened to 10 days. Within 3 court days of this hearing, plaintiffs must stipulate with WC and IP that the three people already deposed in this action (Martin, Ruth Anna Louise and James Granados) have no knowledge supporting plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages or, if they will not so stipulate, must produce the deponents for further deposition no later than June 15, 2016, limited to the issue of punitive damages. WC and IP are allowed to file motions for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages and plaintiffs must stipulate to shortened notice for such motions. The last day to hear such motions, if they are filed, is July 14, 2016 and the Court's order limiting the calendaring of summary motions to four per day in this case does not apply. As of the date of granting plaintiffs' motion, the First Amended Complaint is deemed served on all defendants who have previously appeared. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
